Yellow Scarf.description: A hold item that raises TOUGH in CONTESTS.

Wide Lens.name: Wide Lens
Wide Lens.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a magnifying lens that slightly boosts the accuracy of moves.

Muscle Band.name: Muscle Band
Muscle Band.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a headband that slightly boosts the power of physical moves.

Wise Glasses.name: Wise Glasses
Wise Glasses.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a thick pair of glasses that slightly boosts the power of special moves.

Expert Belt.name: Expert Belt
Expert Belt.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a well-worn belt that slightly boosts the power of supereffective moves.

Light Clay.name: Light Clay
Light Clay.description: A Pokémon hold item that extends the duration of barrier moves like Light Screen and Reflect used by the holder.

Life Orb.name: Life Orb
Life Orb.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts the power of moves, but at the cost of some HP on each hit.

Power Herb.name: Power Herb
Power Herb.description: A single-use item to be held by a Pokémon. It allows the immediate use of a move that charges on the first turn.

Toxic Orb.name: Toxic Orb
Toxic Orb.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a bizarre orb that badly poisons the holder in battle.

Flame Orb.name: Flame Orb
Flame Orb.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a bizarre orb that inflicts a burn on the holder in battle.

Quick Powder.name: Quick Powder
Quick Powder.description: An item to be held by DITTO. Extremely fine yet hard, this odd powder boosts the Speed stat.

Focus Sash.name: Focus Sash
Focus Sash.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. If it has full HP, the holder will endure one potential KO attack, leaving 1 HP.

Zoom Lens.name: Zoom Lens
Zoom Lens.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. If the holder moves after the foe, its critical-hit ratio will be boosted.

Metronome.name: Metronome
Metronome.description: A Pokémon hold item that boosts a move used consecutively. Its effect is reset if another move is used.

Iron Ball.name: Iron Ball
Iron Ball.description: A Pokémon hold item that cuts Speed. It makes Flying-type and levitating holders susceptible to Ground moves.

Lagging Tail.name: Lagging Tail
Lagging Tail.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is tremendously heavy and makes the holder move slower than usual.

Destiny Knot.name: Destiny Knot
Destiny Knot.description: A long, thin, bright red string to be held by a Pokémon. If the holder becomes infatuated, the foe does too.

Black Sludge.name: Black Sludge
Black Sludge.description: A hold item that gradually restores the HP of Poison-type Pokémon. It inflicts damage on all other types.

Icy Rock.name: Icy Rock
Icy Rock.description: A Pokémon hold item that extends the duration of the move Hail used by the holder.

Smooth Rock.name: Smooth Rock
Smooth Rock.description: A Pokémon hold item that extends the duration of the move Sandstorm used by the holder.

Heat Rock.name: Heat Rock
Heat Rock.description: A Pokémon hold item that extends the duration of the move Sunny Day used by the holder.

Damp Rock.name: Damp Rock
Damp Rock.description: A Pokémon hold item that extends the duration of the move Rain Dance used by the holder.

Grip Claw.name: Grip Claw
Grip Claw.description: A Pokémon hold item that extends the duration of multiturn attacks like Bind and Wrap.

Choice Scarf.name: Choice Scarf
Choice Scarf.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This scarf boosts Speed, but allows the use of only one kind of move.

Sticky Barb.name: Sticky Barb
Sticky Barb.description: A hold item that damages the holder on every turn. It may latch on to foes that touch the holder.

Power Bracer.name: Power Bracer
Power Bracer.description: A Pokémon hold item that promotes Attack gain on leveling, but reduces the Speed stat.

Power Belt.name: Power Belt
Power Belt.description: A Pokémon hold item that promotes Defense gain on leveling, but reduces the Speed stat.

Power Lens.name: Power Lens
Power Lens.description: A Pokémon hold item that promotes Sp. Atk gain on leveling, but reduces the Speed stat.

Power Band.name: Power Band
Power Band.description: A Pokémon hold item that promotes Sp. Def gain on leveling, but reduces the Speed stat.

Power Anklet.name: Power Anklet
Power Anklet.description: A Pokémon hold item that promotes Speed gain on leveling, but reduces the Speed stat.

Power Weight.name: Power Weight
Power Weight.description: A Pokémon hold item that promotes HP gain on leveling, but reduces the Speed stat.

Shed Shell.name: Shed Shell
Shed Shell.description: A tough, discarded carapace to be held by a Pokémon. It enables the holder to switch with a waiting Pokémon in battle.

Big Root.name: Big Root
Big Root.description: A Pokémon hold item that boosts the power of HP-stealing moves to let the holder recover more HP.

Choice Specs.name: Choice Specs
Choice Specs.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. These distinctive glasses boost Sp. Atk, but allow only one kind of move to be used.

Flame Plate.name: Flame Plate
Flame Plate.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a stone tablet that boosts the power of Fire-type moves.

Splash Plate.name: Splash Plate
Splash Plate.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a stone tablet that boosts the power of Water-type moves.

Zap Plate.name: Zap Plate
Zap Plate.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a stone tablet that boosts the power of Electric-type moves.

Meadow Plate.name: Meadow Plate
Meadow Plate.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a stone tablet that boosts the power of Grass-type moves.

Icicle Plate.name: Icicle Plate
Icicle Plate.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a stone tablet that boosts the power of Ice-type moves.

Fist Plate.name: Fist Plate
Fist Plate.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a stone tablet that boosts the power of Fighting-type moves.

Toxic Plate.name: Toxic Plate
Toxic Plate.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a stone tablet that boosts the power of Poison-type moves.

Earth Plate.name: Earth Plate
Earth Plate.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a stone tablet that boosts the power of Ground-type moves.

Sky Plate.name: Sky Plate
Sky Plate.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a stone tablet that boosts the power of Flying-type moves.

Mind Plate.name: Mind Plate
Mind Plate.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a stone tablet that boosts the power of Psychic-type moves.

Insect Plate.name: Insect Plate
Insect Plate.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a stone tablet that boosts the power of Bug-type moves.

Stone Plate.name: Stone Plate
Stone Plate.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a stone tablet that boosts the power of Rock-type moves.

Spooky Plate.name: Spooky Plate
Spooky Plate.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a stone tablet that boosts the power of Ghost-type moves.

Draco Plate.name: Draco Plate
Draco Plate.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a stone tablet that boosts the power of Dragon-type moves.

Dread Plate.name: Dread Plate
Dread Plate.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a stone tablet that boosts the power of Dark-type moves.

Iron Plate.name: Iron Plate
Iron Plate.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a stone tablet that boosts the power of Steel-type moves.

Odd Incense.name: Odd Incense
Odd Incense.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is an exotic-smelling incense that boosts the power of Psychic-type moves.

Rock Incense.name: Rock Incense
Rock Incense.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is an exotic-smelling incense that boosts the power of Rock-type moves.

Full Incense.name: Full Incense
Full Incense.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is an exotic-smelling incense that makes the holder bloated and slow moving.

Wave Incense.name: Wave Incense
Wave Incense.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is an exotic-smelling incense that boosts the power of Water-type moves.

Rose Incense.name: Rose Incense
Rose Incense.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is an exotic-smelling incense that boosts the power of Grass-type moves.

Luck Incense.name: Luck Incense
Luck Incense.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It doubles a battle’s prize money if the holding Pokémon joins in.

Pure Incense.name: Pure Incense
Pure Incense.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It helps keep wild Pokémon away if the holder is the first one in the party.

Protector.name: Protector
Protector.description: A protective item of some sort. It is extremely stiff and heavy. It is loved by a certain Pokémon.

Electirizer.name: Electirizer
Electirizer.description: A box packed with a tremendous amount of electric energy. It is loved by a certain Pokémon.

Magmarizer.name: Magmarizer
Magmarizer.description: A box packed with a tremendous amount of magma energy. It is loved by a certain Pokémon.

Dubious Disc.name: Dubious Disc
Dubious Disc.description: A transparent device overflowing with dubious data. Its producer is unknown.

Reaper Cloth.name: Reaper Cloth
Reaper Cloth.description: A cloth imbued with horrifyingly strong spiritual energy. It is loved by a certain Pokémon.

Razor Claw.name: Razor Claw
Razor Claw.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It is a sharply hooked claw that ups the holder’s critical-hit ratio.

Razor Fang.name: Razor Fang
Razor Fang.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. It may cause the foe to flinch when the holder inflicts damage.

Tm01.name: Tm01
Tm01.description: Powerful, but makes the user flinch if hit by the foe.

Tm02.name: Tm02
Tm02.description: Hooks and slashes the foe with long, sharp claws.

Tm03.name: Tm03
Tm03.description: Generates an ultrasonic wave that may confuse.

Tm04.name: Tm04
Tm04.description: Raises SP. ATK and SP. DEF by focusing the mind.

Tm05.name: Tm05
Tm05.description: A savage roar that makes the foe flee  to end the battle.

Tm06.name: Tm06
Tm06.description: Poisons the foe with a toxin that gradually worsens.

Tm07.name: Tm07
Tm07.description: Summons a hailstorm that hurts all types except ICE.

Tm08.name: Tm08
Tm08.description: Bulks up the body to boost both ATTACK & DEFENSE.

Tm09.name: Tm09
Tm09.description: Shoots 2 to 5 seeds in a row to strike the foe.

Tm10.name: Tm10
Tm10.description: The user scatters curious leaves that chase the target. This attack never misses.

Tm11.name: Tm11
Tm11.description: Raises the power of FIRE-type moves for 5 turns.

Tm12.name: Tm12
Tm12.description: Enrages the foe so it can only use attack moves.

Tm13.name: Tm13
Tm13.description: Fires an icy cold beam that may freeze the foe.

Tm14.name: Tm14
Tm14.description: A vicious snow-and- wind attack that may freeze the foe.

Tm15.name: Tm15
Tm15.description: Powerful, but needs recharging the next turn.

Tm16.name: Tm16
Tm16.description: Creates a wall of light that lowers SP. ATK damage.

Tm17.name: Tm17
Tm17.description: Negates all damage, but may fail if used in succession.

Tm18.name: Tm18
Tm18.description: Raises the power of WATER-type moves for 5 turns.

Tm19.name: Tm19
Tm19.description: Recovers half the HP of the damage  this move inflicts.

Tm20.name: Tm20
Tm20.description: Prevents status abnormality with a mystical power.

Tm21.name: Tm21
Tm21.description: The less the user likes you, the more powerful this move.

Tm22.name: Tm22
Tm22.description: Absorbs sunlight in the 1st turn, then attacks next turn.

Tm23.name: Tm23
Tm23.description: Slams the foe with a hard tail. It may lower DEFENSE.

Tm24.name: Tm24
Tm24.description: A powerful electric attack that may cause paralysis.

Tm25.name: Tm25
Tm25.description: Strikes the foe with a thunderbolt. It may paralyze.

Tm26.name: Tm26
Tm26.description: Causes a quake that has no effect on flying foes.

Tm27.name: Tm27
Tm27.description: The more the user likes you, the more powerful this move.

Tm28.name: Tm28
Tm28.description: Digs underground the 1st turn, then strikes next turn.

Tm29.name: Tm29
Tm29.description: A powerful psychic attack that may lower SP. DEF.

Tm30.name: Tm30
Tm30.description: Hurls a dark lump at the foe. It may lower SP. DEF.

Tm31.name: Tm31
Tm31.description: Destroys barriers like LIGHT SCREEN and causes damage.

Tm32.name: Tm32
Tm32.description: Creates illusory copies to enhance elusiveness.

Tm33.name: Tm33
Tm33.description: Creates a wall of light that weakens physical attacks.

Tm34.name: Tm34
Tm34.description: Zaps the foe with a jolt of electricity that never misses.

Tm35.name: Tm35
Tm35.description: Looses a stream of fire that may burn the foe.

Tm36.name: Tm36
Tm36.description: Hurls sludge at the foe. It may poison the foe.

Tm37.name: Tm37
Tm37.description: Causes a sandstorm that hits the foe over several turns.

Tm38.name: Tm38
Tm38.description: A powerful fire attack that may burn the foe.

Tm39.name: Tm39
Tm39.description: Stops the foe from moving with rocks. May lower SPEED.

Tm40.name: Tm40
Tm40.description: An extremely fast attack that can’t be avoided.

Tm41.name: Tm41
Tm41.description: Prevents the foe from using the same move in a row.

Tm42.name: Tm42
Tm42.description: Raises ATTACK when poisoned, burned, or paralyzed.

Tm43.name: Tm43
Tm43.description: Adds an effect to attack depending on the location.

Tm44.name: Tm44
Tm44.description: The user sleeps for 2 turns to restore health and status.

Tm45.name: Tm45
Tm45.description: Makes it tough to attack a foe of the opposite gender.

Tm46.name: Tm46
Tm46.description: While attacking, it may steal the foe’s held item.

Tm47.name: Tm47
Tm47.description: The user makes a swift attack on the target’s legs, which lowers the target’s Speed stat.

Tm48.name: Tm48
Tm48.description: Switches abilities with the foe on the turn this is used.

Tm49.name: Tm49
Tm49.description: Steals the effects of the move the foe is trying to use.

Tm50.name: Tm50
Tm50.description: Enables full-power attack, but sharply lowers SP. ATK.

Tm51.name: Tm51
Tm51.description: The user lands and rests its body. It restores the user’s HP by up to half of its top HP.

Tm52.name: Tm52
Tm52.description: The user attacks at full power. It may also lower the foe’s Sp. Def stat if it hits.

Tm53.name: Tm53
Tm53.description: The user draws power from nature and fires it at the foe. It may also lower the target’s Sp. Def.

Tm54.name: Tm54
Tm54.description: A restrained attack that prevents the foe from fainting. The target is left with at least 1 HP.

Tm55.name: Tm55
Tm55.description: If the target’s HP is half or less, this attack will hit with double the power.

Tm56.name: Tm56
Tm56.description: The user flings its held item at the foe to attack. Its power and effects depend on the item.

Tm57.name: Tm57
Tm57.description: The user attacks with an electric charge. The user may use any remaining electricity to raise its Sp. Atk stat.

Tm58.name: Tm58
Tm58.description: The user endures any attack, leaving 1 HP. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

Tm59.name: Tm59
Tm59.description: The foe is attacked with a shock wave generated by the user’s gaping mouth.

Tm60.name: Tm60
Tm60.description: An energy-draining punch. The user’s HP is restored by half the damage taken by the target.

Tm61.name: Tm61
Tm61.description: The user shoots a sinister, bluish white flame at the foe to inflict a burn.

Tm62.name: Tm62
Tm62.description: The foe is attacked with powdery scales blown by wind. It may also raise all the user’s stats.

Tm63.name: Tm63
Tm63.description: It prevents the foe from using its held item. Its Trainer is also prevented from using items on it.

Tm64.name: Tm64
Tm64.description: The user attacks everything around it by causing a tremendous explosion. The user faints upon using this move.

Tm65.name: Tm65
Tm65.description: The user slashes with a sharp claw made from shadows. It has a high critical-hit ratio.

Tm66.name: Tm66
Tm66.description: The user stores power, then attacks. If the user can use this attack after the foe, its power is doubled.

Tm67.name: Tm67
Tm67.description: The user recycles a single-use item that has been used in battle so it can be used again.

Tm68.name: Tm68
Tm68.description: The user charges the foe using every bit of its power. It must rest on the next turn to recover.

Tm69.name: Tm69
Tm69.description: The user polishes its body to reduce drag. It sharply raises the Speed stat.

Tm70.name: Tm70
Tm70.description: The user flashes a light that cuts the foe’s accuracy. It can also be used to illuminate caves.

Tm71.name: Tm71
Tm71.description: The user stabs the foe with a sharpened stone from below. It has a high critical-hit ratio.

Tm72.name: Tm72
Tm72.description: An attack move that inflicts double the damage if the user has been hurt by the foe in the same turn.

Tm73.name: Tm73
Tm73.description: A weak electric charge is launched at the foe. It causes paralysis if it hits.

Tm74.name: Tm74
Tm74.description: The user tackles the foe from a high-speed spin. The slower the user, the greater the damage.

Tm75.name: Tm75
Tm75.description: A frenetic dance to uplift the fighting spirit. It sharply raises the user’s Attack stat.

Tm76.name: Tm76
Tm76.description: The user lays a trap of levitating stones around the foe. The trap hurts foes that switch into battle.

Tm77.name: Tm77
Tm77.description: The user hypnotizes itself into copying any stat change made by the foe.

Tm78.name: Tm78
Tm78.description: If it is the opposite gender of the user, the foe is charmed into sharply lowering its Sp. Atk stat.

Tm79.name: Tm79
Tm79.description: The user releases a horrible aura imbued with dark thoughts. It may also make the target flinch.

Tm80.name: Tm80
Tm80.description: Large boulders are hurled at the foe to inflict damage. It may also make the target flinch.

Tm81.name: Tm81
Tm81.description: The user slashes at the foe by crossing its scythes or claws as if they were a pair of scissors.

Tm82.name: Tm82
Tm82.description: While it is asleep, the user randomly uses one of the moves it knows.

Tm83.name: Tm83
Tm83.description: The user draws power from the Berry it is holding to attack. The Berry determines its type and power.

Tm84.name: Tm84
Tm84.description: The foe is stabbed with a tentacle or arm seeped with poison. It may also poison the foe.

Tm85.name: Tm85
Tm85.description: An attack that works only on a sleeping foe. It absorbs half the damage caused to heal the user’s HP.

Tm86.name: Tm86
Tm86.description: The user snares the foe with grass and trips it. The heavier the foe, the greater the damage.

Tm87.name: Tm87
Tm87.description: The user enrages the foe into confusion. However, it also sharply raises the foe’s Attack stat.

Tm88.name: Tm88
Tm88.description: The user pecks the foe. If the foe is holding a Berry, the user plucks it and gains its effect.

Tm89.name: Tm89
Tm89.description: After making its attack, the user rushes back to switch places with a party Pokémon in waiting.

Tm90.name: Tm90
Tm90.description: The user makes a copy of itself using some of its HP. The copy serves as the user’s decoy.

Tm91.name: Tm91
Tm91.description: The user gathers all its light energy and releases it in one shot. It may also lower the foe’s Sp. Def stat.

Tm92.name: Tm92
Tm92.description: The user creates a bizarre space in which slower Pokémon get to move first for five turns.

Hm01.name: Hm01
Hm01.description: The foe is cut with a scythe or a claw. It can also be used to cut down thin trees.

Hm02.name: Hm02
Hm02.description: Flies up on the first turn, then attacks next turn.

Hm03.name: Hm03
Hm03.description: Creates a huge wave, then crashes it down on the foe.

Hm04.name: Hm04
Hm04.description: Builds enormous power, then slams the foe.

Hm05.name: Hm05
Hm05.description: Looses a powerful blast of light that reduces accuracy.

Hm06.name: Hm06
Hm06.description: A rock-crushingly tough attack that may lower DEFENSE.

Hm07.name: Hm07
Hm07.description: Attacks the foe with enough power to climb waterfalls.

Hm08.name: Hm08
Hm08.description: Dives underwater the 1st turn, then attacks next turn.

Explorer Kit.name: Explorer Kit
Explorer Kit.description: A bag filled with convenient tools for exploring. It provides access to the Underground.

Loot Sack.name: Loot Sack
Loot Sack.description: A sturdy, spacious bag that is used to carry loot obtained in the coal mine.

Rule Book.name: Rule Book
Rule Book.description: It lists the rules for holding battles. For linked battles, you may choose which set of rules you wish to use.

Poke Radar.name: Poke Radar
Poke Radar.description: A tool that can search out Pokémon that are hiding in grass. Its battery is recharged as you walk.

Point Card.name: Point Card
Point Card.description: A card that lists the Battle Points you have earned.

Journal.name: Journal
Journal.description: A notebook that keeps a day-to-day record of your adventure so far.

Seal Case.name: Seal Case
Seal Case.description: A case for storing Seals that can be applied to the capsule cases of Poké Balls.

Fashion Case.name: Fashion Case
Fashion Case.description: A fancy case for the tidy and organized storage of colorful Pokémon Accessories.

Seal Bag.name: Seal Bag
Seal Bag.description: A tiny bag that can hold ten Seals for decorating Poké Balls.

Pal Pad.name: Pal Pad
Pal Pad.description: A convenient notepad that is used for registering your friends and keeping a record of game play.

Works Key.name: Works Key
Works Key.description: A large key for operating the doors of the Valley Windworks in the canyon. It was held by a Team Galactic Grunt.

Old Charm.name: Old Charm
Old Charm.description: An ancient good-luck charm made of Pokémon bones to be taken to the elder of Celestic Town.

Galactic Key.name: Galactic Key
Galactic Key.description: A card key for disengaging security systems in the Galactic HQ. Losing it can result in punishment, apparently.

Red Chain.name: Red Chain
Red Chain.description: A mythical chain that is said to link the legendary Pokémon that created the Sinnoh region.

Town Map.name: Town Map
Town Map.description: Can be viewed anytime. Shows your present location.

Vs Seeker.name: Vs Seeker
Vs Seeker.description: A rechargeable unit that flags battle- ready TRAINERS.

Coin Case.name: Coin Case
Coin Case.description: A case that holds up to 9,999 COINS.

Old Rod.name: Old Rod
Old Rod.description: Use by any body of water to fish for wild POKéMON.

Good Rod.name: Good Rod
Good Rod.description: A decent fishing rod for catching wild POKéMON.

Super Rod.name: Super Rod
Super Rod.description: The best fishing rod for catching wild POKéMON.

Sprayduck.name: Sprayduck
Sprayduck.description: A watering can shaped like a PSYDUCK. It helps promote healthy growth of Berries planted in soft soil.

Poffin Case.name: Poffin Case
Poffin Case.description: A case for storing Poffin cooked from Berries.

Bicycle.name: Bicycle
Bicycle.description: A folding bicycle that is faster than the RUNNING SHOES.

Suite Key.name: Suite Key
Suite Key.description: A key to one of the suites at the luxury hotel by a lake. For some odd reason, it often disappears.

Oaks Letter.name: Oaks Letter
Oaks Letter.description: A letter from Prof. Oak. Written in it is a request for you to go to Rt. 224.

Lunar Wing.name: Lunar Wing
Lunar Wing.description: A feather that glows like the moon. It is said to possess the power to dispel nightmares.

Member Card.name: Member Card
Member Card.description: A card needed for entering the inn in Canalave City. Oddly, the last date marked on it was 50 years ago.

Azure Flute.name: Azure Flute
Azure Flute.description: A flute that puts out echoing sounds that do not seem to be of this world. It is unknown who made it.

Ss Ticket.name: Ss Ticket
Ss Ticket.description: The ticket required for sailing on a ferry.

Contest Pass.name: Contest Pass
Contest Pass.description: The pass required for entering POKéMON CONTESTS.

Magma Stone.name: Magma Stone
Magma Stone.description: A stone formed when boulders melted in intensely hot magma, then hardened. Magma remains sealed inside.

Parcel.name: Parcel
Parcel.description: A parcel entrusted in your care. You are supposed to deliver it to your childhood friend who left Twinleaf Town.

Coupon 1.name: Coupon 1
Coupon 1.description: A coupon to be exchanged for a Pokémon Watch (Pokétch for short). Three coupons are needed.

Coupon 2.name: Coupon 2
Coupon 2.description: A coupon to be exchanged for a Pokémon Watch (Pokétch for short). Three coupons are needed.

Coupon 3.name: Coupon 3
Coupon 3.description: A coupon to be exchanged for a Pokémon Watch (Pokétch for short). Three coupons are needed.

Storage Key.name: Storage Key
Storage Key.description: The key to the storage inside the ABANDONED SHIP.

Secret Potion.name: Secret Potion
Secret Potion.description: A fantastic medicine dispensed by the pharmacy in Cianwood City. It fully heals a Pokémon of any ailment.

Griseous Orb.name: Griseous Orb
Griseous Orb.description: A glowing orb to be held by GIRATINA. It boosts the power of Dragon- and Ghost-type moves.

Vs Recorder.name: Vs Recorder
Vs Recorder.description: An amazing device that can record a battle either between friends or at a special battle facility.

Gracidea.name: Gracidea
Gracidea.description: A flower sometimes bundled in bouquets to convey gratitude on special occasions like birthdays.

Secret Key.name: Secret Key
Secret Key.description: The key to the CINNABAR ISLAND GYM’s entrance.

Apricorn Box.name: Apricorn Box
Apricorn Box.description: A handy box where you can store up to 99 Apricorns of each kind.

Berry Pots.name: Berry Pots
Berry Pots.description: Handy containers for cultivating Berries wherever you go.

Squirt Bottle.name: Squirt Bottle
Squirt Bottle.description: A bottle used for watering plants in the Berry Pots.

Lure Ball.name: Lure Ball
Lure Ball.description: A Poké Ball for catching Pokémon hooked by a Rod when fishing.

Level Ball.name: Level Ball
Level Ball.description: A Poké Ball for catching Pokémon that are a lower level than your own.

Moon Ball.name: Moon Ball
Moon Ball.description: A Poké Ball for catching Pokémon that evolve using the Moon Stone.

Heavy Ball.name: Heavy Ball
Heavy Ball.description: A Poké Ball for catching very heavy Pokémon.

Fast Ball.name: Fast Ball
Fast Ball.description: A Poké Ball that makes it easier to catch fast Pokémon.

Friend Ball.name: Friend Ball
Friend Ball.description: A Poké Ball that makes caught Pokémon more friendly.

Love Ball.name: Love Ball

Sail Fossil.description: A fossil from a prehistoric Pokémon that once lived on the land. It looks like the impression from a skin sail.

Tm98.name: Tm98
Tm98.description: The user charges at the target and may make it flinch.

Tm99.name: Tm99
Tm99.description: The user damages opposing Pokémon by emitting a powerful flash.

Tm100.name: Tm100
Tm100.description: The user tells the target a secret, and the target loses its ability to concentrate. This lowers the target’s Sp. Atk stat.

Rainbow Flower.description: A flower made of the petals you received from captains. It’s proof that your growth has been recognized by them.

Surge Badge.description: A novelty badge that you can receive at the Kantonian Gym. Rumor has it that it imitates the Badge of a Gym somewhere.

N Lunarizer Split.name: N Lunarizer  Split
N Lunarizer Split.description: A machine to separate Necrozma, which needed light, from Lunala.

Ilimas Normalium Z.name: Ilimas Normalium Z

Left Poke Ball.description: A Poké Ball with a Pokémon that lost its Trainer inside. It seems to be from Ula’ula Island.

Roto Hatch.name: Roto Hatch
Roto Hatch.description: One of the Rotom Powers. It helps Eggs hatch faster.

Roto Bargain.name: Roto Bargain
Roto Bargain.description: One of the Rotom Powers. It reduces the prices of products at Poké Marts by half.

Roto Prize Money.name: Roto Prize Money
Roto Prize Money.description: One of the Rotom Powers. It triples the prize money you receive after battle.

Roto Exp Points.name: Roto Exp Points
Roto Exp Points.description: One of the Rotom Powers. It increases the Exp. Points your Pokémon receive after battle a little.

Roto Friendship.name: Roto Friendship
Roto Friendship.description: One of the Rotom Powers. It helps Pokémon in your party grow friendly faster.

Roto Encounter.name: Roto Encounter
Roto Encounter.description: One of the Rotom Powers. It increases the chance of encountering high-level wild Pokémon a lot for a certain period of time.

Roto Stealth.name: Roto Stealth
Roto Stealth.description: One of the Rotom Powers. It prevents you from encountering wild Pokémon for a certain period of time.

Roto Hp Restore.name: Roto Hp Restore
Roto Hp Restore.description: One of the Rotom Powers. It fully restores the HP of your battling Pokémon.

Roto Pp Restore.name: Roto Pp Restore

Roto Catch.name: Roto Catch
Roto Catch.description: One of the Rotom Powers. It increases the chance to catch Pokémon a lot.

Autograph.name: Autograph
Autograph.description: The autograph you got from the Vermilion City Gym Leader, Lt. Surge. He wrote, “Good luck!”

Golden Razz Berry.name: Golden Razz Berry
Golden Razz Berry.description: A Berry that makes it drastically easier to catch Pokémon when given to them.

Silver Nanab Berry.description: A Berry that calms wild Pokémon you’re trying to catch when given to them.

Endorsement.name: Endorsement
Endorsement.description: A letter of recommendation that you’ll need in order to participate in the Gym Challenge.

Pokemon Box Link.name: Pokemon Box Link
Pokemon Box Link.description: A device that allows you to access the Pokémon storage system. There are some places where it won’t work.

Wishing Star.name: Wishing Star
Wishing Star.description: A stone found in the Galar region with a mysterious power. It’s said that your dreams come true if you find one.

Dynamax Band.name: Dynamax Band
Dynamax Band.description: A Wishing Star has been affixed to it. It lets out a light that allows Pokémon to Dynamax when at a Power Spot.

Fishing Rod Galar.name: Fishing Rod  Galar
Fishing Rod Galar.description: Use it by the water to fish up various kinds of Pokémon.

Rotom Bike.name: Rotom Bike
Rotom Bike.description: This bike can be combined with the Pokémon Rotom to use a turbo boost.

Sausages.name: Sausages
Sausages.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. These sausages should be boiled before eating.

Bobs Food Tin.name: Bobs Food Tin
Bobs Food Tin.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. The tinned food that Bob sells is curiously popular in Galar.

Bachs Food Tin.name: Bachs Food Tin
Bachs Food Tin.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. The tinned food that Bach sells is curiously popular in Galar.

Tin Of Beans.name: Tin Of Beans
Tin Of Beans.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. When simmered, the beans suffuse a dish with their mild flavor.

Bread.name: Bread
Bread.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. An indispensable tool for mopping up that extra curry left on your plate.

Pasta.name: Pasta
Pasta.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. These flour noodles make for a surprisingly good match with curry.

Mixed Mushrooms.name: Mixed Mushrooms
Mixed Mushrooms.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. The chewy texture of the mushrooms transforms any dish they’re added to.

Smoke Poke Tail.name: Smoke Poke Tail
Smoke Poke Tail.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. When a Slowpoke’s tail falls off, it grows back quickly.

Large Leek.name: Large Leek
Large Leek.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. Whether this is the kind of vegetable stalk that Farfetch’d like is unknown.

Fancy Apple.name: Fancy Apple
Fancy Apple.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. An apple chosen above others. It has spectacular form and a brilliant sheen.

Brittle Bones.name: Brittle Bones
Brittle Bones.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. The taste boiled off from the bones is sure to give a dish a light umami taste.

Pack Of Potatoes.name: Pack Of Potatoes
Pack Of Potatoes.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. Potatoes help mellow out a spicy taste, to make a dish more mild.

Pungent Root.name: Pungent Root
Pungent Root.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. Though carelessly picked, these herbs can add a fragrant accent to a dish.

Salad Mix.name: Salad Mix
Salad Mix.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. The assortment of vegetables looks quite appealing and healthy.

Fried Food.name: Fried Food
Fried Food.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. It’s been a while since it was fried, so it’s gotten greasy.

Boiled Egg.name: Boiled Egg
Boiled Egg.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. Even just one boiled egg can really enhance a dish.

Camping Gear.name: Camping Gear
Camping Gear.description: Everything you need to set up a tent and get cooking in a Wild Area or at a camping spot.

Rusted Sword.name: Rusted Sword
Rusted Sword.description: It is said that a hero used this sword to halt a terrible disaster in ancient times. But it’s grown rusty and worn.

Rusted Shield.name: Rusted Shield
Rusted Shield.description: It is said that a hero used this shield to halt a terrible disaster in ancient times. But it’s grown rusty and worn.

Fossilized Bird.name: Fossilized Bird
Fossilized Bird.description: The fossil of an ancient Pokémon that once soared through the sky. What it looked like is a mystery.

Fossilized Fish.name: Fossilized Fish
Fossilized Fish.description: The fossil of an ancient Pokémon that once lived in the sea. What it looked like is a mystery.

Fossilized Drake.name: Fossilized Drake
Fossilized Drake.description: The fossil of an ancient Pokémon that once roamed the land. What it looked like is a mystery.

Fossilized Dino.name: Fossilized Dino
Fossilized Dino.description: The fossil of an ancient Pokémon that once lived in the sea. What it looked like is a mystery.

Strawberry Sweet.name: Strawberry Sweet
Strawberry Sweet.description: A strawberry-shaped sweet. When a Milcery holds this, it will spin around happily.

Love Sweet.name: Love Sweet
Love Sweet.description: A heart-shaped sweet. When a Milcery holds this, it spins around happily.

Berry Sweet.name: Berry Sweet
Berry Sweet.description: A berry-shaped sweet. When a Milcery holds this, it spins around happily.

Clover Sweet.name: Clover Sweet
Clover Sweet.description: A clover-shaped sweet. When a Milcery holds this, it spins around happily.

Flower Sweet.name: Flower Sweet
Flower Sweet.description: A flower-shaped sweet. When a Milcery holds this, it spins around happily.

Star Sweet.name: Star Sweet
Star Sweet.description: A star-shaped sweet. When a Milcery holds this, it spins around happily.

Ribbon Sweet.name: Ribbon Sweet
Ribbon Sweet.description: A ribbon-shaped sweet. When a Milcery holds this, it spins around happily.

Sweet Apple.name: Sweet Apple
Sweet Apple.description: A peculiar apple that can make a certain species of Pokémon evolve. It’s exceptionally sweet.

Tart Apple.name: Tart Apple
Tart Apple.description: A peculiar apple that can make a certain species of Pokémon evolve. It’s exceptionally tart.

Throat Spray.name: Throat Spray
Throat Spray.description: Raises Sp. Atk when a Pokémon uses a sound-based move.

Eject Pack.name: Eject Pack
Eject Pack.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. When the holder’s stats are lowered, it will be switched out of battle.

Heavy Duty Boots.name: Heavy Duty Boots
Heavy Duty Boots.description: These boots prevent the effects of traps set on the battlefield.

Blunder Policy.name: Blunder Policy
Blunder Policy.description: Raises Speed sharply when a Pokémon misses with a move because of accuracy.

Room Service.name: Room Service
Room Service.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. Lowers Speed when Trick Room takes effect.

Utility Umbrella.name: Utility Umbrella
Utility Umbrella.description: An item to be held by a Pokémon. This sturdy umbrella protects the holder from the effects of rain and harsh sunlight.

Exp Candy Xs.name: Exp Candy Xs
Exp Candy Xs.description: A candy that is packed with energy. When consumed, it will grant a single Pokémon a very small amount of Exp. Points.

Exp Candy S.name: Exp Candy S
Exp Candy S.description: A candy that is packed with energy. When consumed, it will grant a single Pokémon a small amount of Exp. Points.

Exp Candy M.name: Exp Candy M
Exp Candy M.description: A candy that is packed with energy. When consumed, it will grant a single Pokémon a moderate amount of Exp. Points.

Exp Candy L.name: Exp Candy L
Exp Candy L.description: A candy that is packed with energy. When consumed, it will grant a single Pokémon a large amount of Exp. Points.

Exp Candy Xl.name: Exp Candy Xl
Exp Candy Xl.description: A candy that is packed with energy. When consumed, it will grant a single Pokémon a very large amount of Exp. Points.

Dynamax Candy.name: Dynamax Candy
Dynamax Candy.description: A candy that is packed with energy. When consumed, it will raise the Dynamax Level of a single Pokémon by one. A higher level means higher HP when Dynamaxed.

Tr00.name: Tr00
Tr00.description: A frenetic dance to uplift the fighting spirit. This sharply raises the user’s Attack stat.

Tr01.name: Tr01
Tr01.description: The user drops onto the target with its full body weight. This may also leave the target with paralysis.

Tr02.name: Tr02
Tr02.description: The target is scorched with an intense blast of fire. This may also leave the target with a burn.

Tr03.name: Tr03
Tr03.description: The target is blasted by a huge volume of water launched under great pressure.

Tr04.name: Tr04
Tr04.description: The user attacks everything around it by swamping its surroundings with a giant wave.

Tr05.name: Tr05
Tr05.description: The target is struck with an icy-cold beam of energy. This may also leave the target frozen.

Tr06.name: Tr06
Tr06.description: A howling blizzard is summoned to strike opposing Pokémon. This may also leave the opposing Pokémon frozen.

Tr07.name: Tr07
Tr07.description: A powerful low kick that makes the target fall over. The heavier the target, the greater the move’s power.

Tr08.name: Tr08
Tr08.description: A strong electric blast crashes down on the target. This may also leave the target with paralysis.

Tr09.name: Tr09
Tr09.description: A wicked thunderbolt is dropped on the target to inflict damage. This may also leave the target with paralysis.

Tr10.name: Tr10
Tr10.description: The user sets off an earthquake that strikes every Pokémon around it.

Tr11.name: Tr11
Tr11.description: The target is hit by a strong telekinetic force. This may also lower the target’s Sp. Def stat.

Tr12.name: Tr12
Tr12.description: The user relaxes and lightens its body to move faster. This sharply raises the Speed stat.

Tr13.name: Tr13
Tr13.description: The user takes a deep breath and focuses so that critical hits land more easily.

Tr14.name: Tr14
Tr14.description: The user waggles a finger and stimulates its brain into randomly using nearly any move.

Tr15.name: Tr15
Tr15.description: The target is attacked with an intense blast of all-consuming fire. This may also leave the target with a burn.

Tr16.name: Tr16
Tr16.description: The user charges at the target and may make it flinch.

Tr17.name: Tr17
Tr17.description: The user temporarily empties its mind to forget its concerns. This sharply raises the user’s Sp. Def stat.

Tr18.name: Tr18
Tr18.description: The user drains the target’s blood. The user’s HP is restored by half the damage taken by the target.

Tr19.name: Tr19
Tr19.description: The user strikes with a simultaneous three-beam attack. This may also burn, freeze, or paralyze the target.

Tr20.name: Tr20
Tr20.description: The user creates a substitute for itself using some of its HP. The substitute serves as the user’s decoy.

Tr21.name: Tr21
Tr21.description: An all-out attack that becomes more powerful the less HP the user has.

Tr22.name: Tr22
Tr22.description: Unsanitary sludge is hurled at the target. This may also poison the target.

Tr23.name: Tr23
Tr23.description: The user lays a trap of spikes at the opposing team’s feet. The trap hurts Pokémon that switch into battle.

Tr24.name: Tr24
Tr24.description: The user rampages and attacks for two to three turns. The user then becomes confused.

Tr25.name: Tr25
Tr25.description: The user materializes an odd psychic wave to attack the target. This attack does physical damage.

Tr26.name: Tr26
Tr26.description: The user endures any attack with at least 1 HP. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

Tr27.name: Tr27
Tr27.description: While it is asleep, the user randomly uses one of the moves it knows.

Tr28.name: Tr28
Tr28.description: Using its tough and impressive horn, the user rams into the target with no letup.

Tr29.name: Tr29
Tr29.description: The user switches places with a party Pokémon in waiting and passes along any stat changes.

Tr30.name: Tr30
Tr30.description: The user compels the target to keep using the move it encored for three turns.

Tr31.name: Tr31
Tr31.description: The target is slammed with a steel-hard tail. This may also lower the target’s Defense stat.

Tr32.name: Tr32
Tr32.description: The user crunches up the target with sharp fangs. This may also lower the target’s Defense stat.

Tr33.name: Tr33
Tr33.description: The user hurls a shadowy blob at the target. This may also lower the target’s Sp. Def stat.

Tr34.name: Tr34
Tr34.description: Two turns after this move is used, a hunk of psychic energy attacks the target.

Tr35.name: Tr35
Tr35.description: The user attacks in an uproar for three turns. During that time, no Pokémon can fall asleep.

Tr36.name: Tr36
Tr36.description: The user attacks by exhaling hot breath on opposing Pokémon. This may also leave those Pokémon with a burn.

Tr37.name: Tr37
Tr37.description: The target is taunted into a rage that allows it to use only attack moves for three turns.

Tr38.name: Tr38
Tr38.description: The user catches the target off guard and swaps its held item with its own.

Tr39.name: Tr39
Tr39.description: The user attacks the target with great power. However, this also lowers the user’s Attack and Defense stats.

Tr40.name: Tr40
Tr40.description: The user employs its psychic power to exchange Abilities with the target.

Tr41.name: Tr41
Tr41.description: The user launches a kick that lands a critical hit more easily. This may also leave the target with a burn.

Tr42.name: Tr42
Tr42.description: The user lets loose a horribly echoing shout with the power to inflict damage.

Tr43.name: Tr43
Tr43.description: The user attacks the target at full power. The attack’s recoil harshly lowers the user’s Sp. Atk stat.

Tr44.name: Tr44
Tr44.description: The user absorbs a mystical power from space to raise its Defense and Sp. Def stats.

Tr45.name: Tr45
Tr45.description: The user attacks by shooting muddy water at opposing Pokémon. This may also lower their accuracy.

Tr46.name: Tr46
Tr46.description: The user hardens its body’s surface like iron, sharply raising its Defense stat.

Tr47.name: Tr47
Tr47.description: The user slashes the target with huge sharp claws.

Tr48.name: Tr48
Tr48.description: The user tenses its muscles to bulk up its body, raising both its Attack and Defense stats.

Tr49.name: Tr49
Tr49.description: The user quietly focuses its mind and calms its spirit to raise its Sp. Atk and Sp. Def stats.

Tr50.name: Tr50
Tr50.description: The user handles a sharp leaf like a sword and attacks by cutting its target. Critical hits land more easily.

Tr51.name: Tr51
Tr51.description: The user vigorously performs a mystic, powerful dance that raises its Attack and Speed stats.

Tr52.name: Tr52
Tr52.description: The user tackles the target with a high-speed spin. The slower the user compared to the target, the greater the move’s power.

Tr53.name: Tr53
Tr53.description: The user fights the target up close without guarding itself. This also lowers the user’s Defense and Sp. Def stats.

Tr54.name: Tr54
Tr54.description: The user lays a trap of poison spikes at the feet of the opposing team. The spikes will poison opposing Pokémon that switch into battle.

Tr55.name: Tr55
Tr55.description: The user cloaks itself in fire and charges the target. This also damages the user quite a lot. This attack may leave the target with a burn.

Tr56.name: Tr56
Tr56.description: The user lets loose a blast of aura power from deep within its body at the target. This attack never misses.

Tr57.name: Tr57
Tr57.description: The target is stabbed with a tentacle, arm, or the like steeped in poison. This may also poison the target.

Tr58.name: Tr58
Tr58.description: The user releases a horrible aura imbued with dark thoughts. This may also make the target flinch.

Tr59.name: Tr59
Tr59.description: The user slams a barrage of hard-shelled seeds down on the target from above.

Tr60.name: Tr60
Tr60.description: The user slashes at the target by crossing its scythes or claws as if they were a pair of scissors.

Tr61.name: Tr61
Tr61.description: The user generates a damaging sound wave by vibration. This may also lower the target’s Sp. Def stat.

Tr62.name: Tr62
Tr62.description: The target is attacked with a shock wave generated by the user’s gaping mouth.

Tr63.name: Tr63
Tr63.description: The user attacks with a ray of light that sparkles as if it were made of gemstones.

Tr64.name: Tr64
Tr64.description: The user heightens its mental focus and unleashes its power. This may also lower the target’s Sp. Def stat.

Tr65.name: Tr65
Tr65.description: The user draws power from nature and fires it at the target. This may also lower the target’s Sp. Def stat.

Tr66.name: Tr66
Tr66.description: The user tucks in its wings and charges from a low altitude. This also damages the user quite a lot.

Tr67.name: Tr67
Tr67.description: The user makes the ground under the target erupt with power. This may also lower the target’s Sp. Def stat.

Tr68.name: Tr68
Tr68.description: The user stimulates its brain by thinking bad thoughts. This sharply raises the user’s Sp. Atk stat.

Tr69.name: Tr69
Tr69.description: The user focuses its willpower to its head and attacks the target. This may also make the target flinch.

Tr70.name: Tr70
Tr70.description: The user gathers all its light energy and releases it all at once. This may also lower the target’s Sp. Def stat.

Tr71.name: Tr71
Tr71.description: The user whips up a storm of leaves around the target. The attack’s recoil harshly lowers the user’s Sp. Atk stat.

Tr72.name: Tr72
Tr72.description: The user violently whirls its vines, tentacles, or the like to harshly lash the target.

Tr73.name: Tr73
Tr73.description: The user shoots filthy garbage at the target to attack. This may also poison the target.

Tr74.name: Tr74
Tr74.description: The user slams the target with its steel-hard head. This may also make the target flinch.

Tr75.name: Tr75
Tr75.description: The user stabs the target from below with sharpened stones. Critical hits land more easily.

Tr76.name: Tr76
Tr76.description: The user lays a trap of levitating stones around the opposing team. The trap hurts opposing Pokémon that switch into battle.

Tr77.name: Tr77
Tr77.description: The user snares the target with grass and trips it. The heavier the target, the greater the move’s power.

Tr78.name: Tr78
Tr78.description: The user strikes everything around it by swamping the area with a giant sludge wave. This may also poison those hit.

Tr79.name: Tr79
Tr79.description: The user slams into the target with its heavy body. The more the user outweighs the target, the greater the move’s power.

Tr80.name: Tr80
Tr80.description: The user hurls an electric orb at the target. The faster the user is than the target, the greater the move’s power.

Tr81.name: Tr81
Tr81.description: The user turns the target’s power against it. The higher the target’s Attack stat, the greater the damage it deals.

Tr82.name: Tr82
Tr82.description: The user attacks the target with stored power. The more the user’s stats are raised, the greater the move’s power.

Tr83.name: Tr83
Tr83.description: The user teleports using a strange power and switches places with one of its allies.

Tr84.name: Tr84
Tr84.description: The user shoots boiling hot water at its target. This may also leave the target with a burn.

Tr85.name: Tr85
Tr85.description: The user is roused, and its Attack and Sp. Atk stats increase.

Tr86.name: Tr86
Tr86.description: The user shrouds itself in electricity and smashes into its target. This also damages the user a little.

Tr87.name: Tr87
Tr87.description: The user crashes into its target while rotating its body like a drill. Critical hits land more easily.

Tr88.name: Tr88
Tr88.description: The user slams its target with its flame-covered body. The more the user outweighs the target, the greater the move’s power.

Tr89.name: Tr89
Tr89.description: The user attacks by wrapping its opponent in a fierce wind that flies up into the sky. This may also confuse the target.

Tr90.name: Tr90
Tr90.description: The user plays rough with the target and attacks it. This may also lower the target’s Attack stat.

Tr91.name: Tr91
Tr91.description: Opposing Pokémon are drenched in an odd poisonous liquid. This lowers the Attack, Sp. Atk, and Speed stats of a poisoned target.

Tr92.name: Tr92
Tr92.description: The user damages opposing Pokémon by emitting a powerful flash.

Tr93.name: Tr93
Tr93.description: The user swings both arms and hits the target. The target’s stat changes don’t affect this attack’s damage.

Tr94.name: Tr94
Tr94.description: The user fiercely attacks the target using its entire body.

Tr95.name: Tr95
Tr95.description: The user attacks the target’s throat, and the resultant suffering prevents the target from using moves that emit sound for two turns.

Tr96.name: Tr96
Tr96.description: The user attacks the enemy with a pollen puff that explodes. If the target is an ally, it gives the ally a pollen puff that restores its HP instead.

Tr97.name: Tr97
Tr97.description: The user bites the target with its psychic capabilities. This can also destroy Light Screen and Reflect.

Tr98.name: Tr98
Tr98.description: The user slams into the target using a full-force blast of water. This may also lower the target’s Defense stat.

Tr99.name: Tr99
Tr99.description: The user attacks by slamming its body into the target. The higher the user’s Defense, the more damage it can inflict on the target.

Tm00.name: Tm00
Tm00.description: The target is slugged by a punch thrown with muscle-packed power.

Lonely Mint.description: When a Pokémon smells this mint, its Attack will grow more easily, but its Defense will grow more slowly.

Serious Mint.name: Serious Mint
Serious Mint.description: When a Pokémon smells this mint, all of its stats will grow at an equal rate.

Wishing Piece.name: Wishing Piece
Wishing Piece.description: Throw one into a Pokémon Den to attract a wild Dynamax Pokémon.

Cracked Pot.name: Cracked Pot
Cracked Pot.description: A peculiar teapot that can make a certain species of Pokémon evolve. It may be cracked, but tea poured from it is delicious.

Chipped Pot.name: Chipped Pot
Chipped Pot.description: A peculiar teapot that can make a certain species of Pokémon evolve. It may be chipped, but tea poured from it is delicious.

Hi Tech Earbuds.name: Hi Tech Earbuds
Hi Tech Earbuds.description: Strange earbuds that allow you to freely control the volume of various sounds.

Fruit Bunch.name: Fruit Bunch
Fruit Bunch.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. The syrupy fruits give off a strong tropical feel.

Moomoo Cheese.name: Moomoo Cheese
Moomoo Cheese.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. Melted cheese can really give a curry some richness.

Spice Mix.name: Spice Mix
Spice Mix.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. There are over fifty spices contained within this ingredient’s hot flavor.

Fresh Cream.name: Fresh Cream
Fresh Cream.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. Add some whipped cream to a spicy curry to give it some sweetness.

Packaged Curry.name: Packaged Curry
Packaged Curry.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. Using packaged curry helps make sure there are no slipups when cooking.

Coconut Milk.name: Coconut Milk
Coconut Milk.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. The refined sweetness of this coconut milk makes it quite popular.

Instant Noodles.name: Instant Noodles
Instant Noodles.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. It has a junk-food sort of taste, but it goes well with curry.

Precooked Burger.name: Precooked Burger
Precooked Burger.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. If you’re unsure about what to cook, you can’t go wrong with some burger.

Gigantamix.name: Gigantamix
Gigantamix.description: One of the many ingredients that can be used for cooking at your camp. A mysterious spice. Even a tiny sprinkle is sure to make a curry gigantic.

Wishing Chip.name: Wishing Chip
Wishing Chip.description: Toss a number of them into a Pokémon Den for the chance to attract a wild Dynamax Pokémon.

Rotom Bike Water Mode.name: Rotom Bike  Water Mode
Rotom Bike Water Mode.description: This bike can be combined with the Pokémon Rotom to not only do a turbo boost but also run on the water.

Catching Charm.name: Catching Charm
Catching Charm.description: Holding it is said to increase the chance of getting a critical catch. Curiously, the charm doesn’t shake much.

Old Letter.name: Old Letter
Old Letter.description: A letter entrusted to you by a girl, to be delivered to a boy. Strangely, it looks old to you.

Band Autograph.name: Band Autograph
Band Autograph.description: It’s signed by all members of the Galar region’s most charismatic band, the Maximizers.

Sonias Book.name: Sonias Book
Sonias Book.description: Professor Sonia’s published writings. Her new discoveries about the Galar region’s legends are recorded in this enjoyable read.

Rotom Catalog.name: Rotom Catalog
Rotom Catalog.description: A catalog of devices that Rotom like. Use the catalog to have Rotom hop in and out of the various devices listed within.

Max Honey.name: Max Honey
Max Honey.description: Honey that Dynamax Vespiquen produces. Adding this honey to Max Soup makes the taste very smooth. It also has the same effect as a Max Revive.

Max Mushrooms.name: Max Mushrooms
Max Mushrooms.description: Mushrooms that have the power of changing Dynamax forms. They boost all stats of a Pokémon during battle.

Galarica Twig.name: Galarica Twig
Galarica Twig.description: A twig from a species of tree called Galarica that grows in certain areas around the Galar region. It’s a material for an accessory for a certain Pokémon.

Galarica Cuff.name: Galarica Cuff
Galarica Cuff.description: A cuff made from woven-together Galarica Twigs. Giving it to Galarian Slowpoke makes the Pokémon very happy.

Style Card.name: Style Card
Style Card.description: A card that increases the selection in boutiques and hair salons.

Armor Pass.name: Armor Pass
Armor Pass.description: A pass to go to the Isle of Armor. Show it to the rail staff at Wedgehurst Station.

Rotom Bike Sparkling White.name: Rotom Bike  Sparkling White
Rotom Bike Sparkling White.description: This bike can do a turbo boost and run on the water using Rotom’s power. It’s a sparkling white that shines brilliantly.

Rotom Bike Glistening Black.name: Rotom Bike  Glistening Black
Rotom Bike Glistening Black.description: This bike can do a turbo boost and run on the water using Rotom’s power. It’s a glistening black that shines reddish-black.

Exp Charm.name: Exp Charm
Exp Charm.description: A charm that increases the Exp. Points that Pokémon can get. A machine-like object is inside the charm.

Armorite Ore.name: Armorite Ore
Armorite Ore.description: Rare ore that can be obtained on the Isle of Armor. Something good will happen if you give it to a collector on the Isle of Armor.

Mark Charm.name: Mark Charm
Mark Charm.description: A mysterious and unique charm that increases the chance of finding a Pokémon with a mark.

Reins Of Unity Merge.name: Reins Of Unity  Merge
Reins Of Unity Merge.description: Reins that people presented to the king. They enhance Calyrex’s power over bountiful harvests and unite Calyrex with its beloved steed.

Reins Of Unity Split.name: Reins Of Unity  Split
Reins Of Unity Split.description: Reins that people presented to the king. They enable those who are connected to communicate with each other. The reins separate Calyrex and its beloved steed.

Galarica Wreath.name: Galarica Wreath
Galarica Wreath.description: A wreath made from woven-together Galarica Twigs. Placing it on the head of a Galarian Slowpoke makes the Pokémon very happy.

Legendary Clue 1.name: Legendary Clue 1
Legendary Clue 1.description: A clue about the King of Bountiful Harvests. It’s written by Peony, and an old photo is attached to it.

Legendary Clue 2.name: Legendary Clue 2
Legendary Clue 2.description: A clue about the legend of giants. It’s written by Peony and has unique drawings.

Legendary Clue 3.name: Legendary Clue 3
Legendary Clue 3.description: A clue about the legend of bird Pokémon. It’s written by Peony and has a unique drawing.

Legendary Clue Question.name: Legendary Clue Question
Legendary Clue Question.description: A mysterious clue that Peony seems to have dropped. There’s a photo attached to it showing a shining hole opening above the Max Lair.

Crown Pass.name: Crown Pass
Crown Pass.description: A pass to go to the Crown Tundra. Show it to the rail staff at Wedgehurst Station.

Wooden Crown.name: Wooden Crown
Wooden Crown.description: A mysterious object made of wood. Part of it seems to be chipped. Maybe it’s meant to be part of something.

Radiant Petal.name: Radiant Petal
Radiant Petal.description: A single petal that the King of Bountiful Harvests gave you. It radiates faint light.

White Mane Hair.name: White Mane Hair
White Mane Hair.description: Mane hair that belonged to the King of Bountiful Harvests’ beloved steed, Glastrier. The hair is strong and very flexible.

Black Mane Hair.name: Black Mane Hair
Black Mane Hair.description: Mane hair that belonged to the King of Bountiful Harvests’ beloved steed, Spectrier. The hair is very smooth to the touch.

Iceroot Carrot.name: Iceroot Carrot
Iceroot Carrot.description: Produce that the King of Bountiful Harvests’ beloved steed likes to eat. It grew in a field covered in snow.

Shaderoot Carrot.name: Shaderoot Carrot
Shaderoot Carrot.description: Produce that the King of Bountiful Harvests’ beloved steed likes to eat. It grew in a gloomy field.

Dynite Ore.name: Dynite Ore
Dynite Ore.description: Mysterious ore that can be obtained in the Max Lair. Something good will happen if you give it to a collector in the Crown Tundra.

Carrot Seeds.name: Carrot Seeds
Carrot Seeds.description: Seeds to plant in a field. The kind of carrot you’ll get is determined by which field you plant the seeds in.

Ability Patch.name: Ability Patch
Ability Patch.description: A patch that allows a Pokémon with a regular Ability to have a rare Ability.

Reins Of Unity.name: Reins Of Unity
Reins Of Unity.description: Cords that shine when you hold them up to the light. The people of old times presented these to the King of Bountiful Harvests to express their gratitude.

Laheavy Ball.name: Laheavy Ball
Laheavy Ball.description: Doesn't fly far, but is more effective if the Pokémon hasn't noticed the player.

Laleaden Ball.name: Laleaden Ball
Laleaden Ball.description: Upgraded version of the Heavy Ball. Doesn't fly far, but is more effective if the Pokémon hasn't noticed the player.

Lagigaton Ball.name: Lagigaton Ball
Lagigaton Ball.description: Upgraded version of the Leaden Ball. Doesn't fly far, but is more effective if the Pokémon hasn't noticed the player.

Lafeather Ball.name: Lafeather Ball
Lafeather Ball.description: Can be thrown further than a regular Poké Ball. Is more effective for catching Pokémon that fly high in the air.

Lawing Ball.name: Lawing Ball
Lawing Ball.description: Can be thrown further than a Feather Ball. Is more effective for catching Pokémon that fly high in the air.

Lajet Ball.name: Lajet Ball
Lajet Ball.description: Can be thrown further than a Wing Ball. Is more effective for catching Pokémon that fly high in the air.
